author: Flavio Azurita
description: Turn plain metadata values into beautiful color-coded pills with icons вҖ” for properties, Bases tables, and cards. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Flavio Azurita
manifest.description: Create attractive and customizable status indicators with Lucide icons for your metadata properties.
manifest.name: Typify

name: Typify

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.5.2 рҹҡҖ

This is a hotfix update addressing strict security guidelines from the Obsidian Plugin Review process.

## вҡҷпёҸ Architecture & Security
- **Strict DOM Parsing:** Completely eliminated the use of `<template>` and `innerHTML` across the plugin's codebase. SVG rendering now exclusively utilizes `DOMParser` for safe, strict XML parsing, ensuring 100% compliance with Obsidian's `@microsoft/sdl/no-inner-html` security policy and preventing any potential injection vectors.

> [!Note]
> To see all the major features and visual updates introduced in the 1.5.0 update, please check the previous release notes below.

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.5.1 рҹҡҖ

This is a technical update focused on optimizing the plugin's core performance, memory management, and compatibility with new Obsidian features. Many of the architectural changes in this release were implemented to strictly comply with the **Obsidian Plugin Review Bot** guidelines, ensuring the highest standards of security and performance for our users.

## вҡҷпёҸ Architecture, Optimizations & Security

- **Zero Memory Leaks:** Completely refactored the internal rendering engine for Modals and Settings. Replaced `innerHTML` usage with inert `<template>` element parsing, preventing memory leaks in the V8 engine and ensuring security during massive JSON imports, fully complying with Obsidian's strict DOM manipulation guidelines.
- **Popout Window Support:** Upgraded DOM interactions to use `activeDocument`, guaranteeing that the plugin works flawlessly across multiple independent floating windows (Popouts) in Obsidian as requested by the plugin review team.
- **Instant Reactivity:** The DOM Manager now correctly identifies and reprocesses previously unstyled pills when a new style is imported, updating the UI instantly without requiring the user to click or switch notes.
- **Blazing Fast Imports:** Fixed a race condition and improved the CSS cache building sequence. Custom SVGs and Favicons now load synchronously during bulk settings import.
- **TypeScript & Build:** Extracted native Obsidian typings into dedicated modules. Raised the minimum Obsidian version (`minAppVersion`) to `1.8.7` to support the latest native typings.

## рҹҢҗ Localization

- Added new translation keys for the link placeholder across all 5 supported languages.

> [!Note]
> To see all the major features and visual updates introduced in the 1.5.0 update, please check the previous release notes below.

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.5.0 рҹҡҖ

We are very excited to introduce Typify 1.5.0! This update brings some of the most requested features by the community, along with a major visual modernization in the settings panel and a huge expansion to our color support and website icons (favicons).


## вңЁ What's New & Features

### рҹҺЁ Color Palette
The plugin's color management has been expanded. What used to be just a basic color section is now a fully manageable palette.

- **Smart Harmony Generator:** A simple and easy way to automatically create palettes from a base color, using harmony rules (analogous, complementary, gradient/shades, or random) with real-time preview.

- **My Colors:** Save up to 15 custom colors, integrated with the system's native color picker.

This one is for you, litaliaa! :3


### рҹҢҗ Manage Favicons (Local Link Icons)

In the past update, support for link styling was added. Now, the functionality to use website favicons to customize your Typify tags has been added.

- Added a panel to manage favicons, allowing you to clear the cache or renew failed icons and check their status. With it, you can change the favicon provider in case the default one doesn't work for a specific site.

- Added a **secure local cache** for URL-based favicons. This way, the plugin doesn't need to make a request to the website every time it uses the favicon, optimizing performance.

To use it, just make sure the **Associated links** option is enabled. Then, in the **Create style** modal, under the URL field, you can add a link and click the button to fetch its favicon.

It took a while, but it's here, ReconVirus! :D

> [!Important]  
> It's not always possible to fetch a website's exact favicon. This is a very common limitation, since websites don't always make their favicons available natively or easily accessible.  

### рҹ“° Built-in Changelog Board

A new way to see what changed in Typify! And best of all, intuitively and in your own language. 

- The interface displays the release notes in the user's native language, separated by new features, improvements, fixes, and breaking changes.

### рҹҡ© Plugin Notices

The descriptive texts for the settings have been simplified. Technical warnings, limitations, and information have been consolidated into a new centralized notices panel. What used to be scattered across multiple settings is now gathered in a single place so users can get an overview of all the plugin's relevant information.

### рҹҳ¶вҖҚрҹҢ«пёҸ Reveal the Close Button

Sometimes it's hard to either have something or not have it without a middle ground. This is the case with the tag close buttons. That's why now you'll have a new option to access the tag's `X` button even when it's hidden.

Thank you, Moyf! :D

---

> [!Important]  
> The **Custom color palette** and **Manage favicons** features currently appear with an Experimental label, but I tested them thoroughly. I only labeled them as such because, even though they are mature, these features need more usage time so I can validate how well they behave over time and across many users. 


## рҹ–ҢпёҸ Visual Redesign & UX

- **Interactive Forms in Cards:** The dropdown selection lists for Shape and Color Mode have been replaced with more intuitive and pleasant-to-use cards. The cards utilize the active theme's accent color.

    The **Color palette** and **Favicon manager** modals also use this new, more pleasant visual style.

- **Native Transitions and Feedback:** Optimized transitions in the settings menus to avoid full interface reloads (visual re-renders). Modals and panels have been adjusted to follow Obsidian's native visual patterns and spacing.

- **Style Preview**: The live preview of the tag style being created has been simplified. It saves space while maintaining its intuitive use.

- **Text Refinement**: The descriptive texts for some settings have been simplified to make them easier to understand.

- **Panel Refinement**: Some panels have been adjusted to inherit the high-contrast primary color.


## вҡҷпёҸ Architecture, Optimizations & Cleanup

- **CSS Modularization:** The main CSS file was modularized into 15 partial files with the build managed via *esbuild*. The legacy `csi-` prefix was globally replaced by `typify-`.

- **TypeScript and Obsidian Compliance:** ESLint and TypeScript configurations were aligned to the strict checking standards (`Strict TypeChecked`) used in Obsidian's official repository.

- **Internationalization (i18n):** Language support (Spanish, French, Brazilian Portuguese, Chinese, and English) was synchronized and standardized. Obsolete translation keys and hardcoded strings were removed from the source code.


## рҹӣ пёҸ Bug Fixes


- The export and import format now covers all user preferences, including styles and color palettes. The import process maintains backwards compatibility with backups from previous versions.

- Fixed a silent failure when importing styles with associated URL properties; URLs are no longer ignored by the validation routine.

- Resolved a CSS rendering bug in the "Bases Cards" component where the favicon received an unwanted fill background (`currentColor`).

And muuuuuuuuuuuuuuch more!


### рҹ’– To The Community

There are more things being planned and I am very grateful for every suggestion, idea, feedback, and bug report. 
You guys are awesome! :D

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.4.0 рҹҡҖ

We are very excited to introduce Typify **1.4.0**! This update brings two of the most requested features by the community, along with a major visual modernization in the settings panel and a huge expansion to our icon library.

## вңЁ New Features

- **рҹ”— Associated Links:** Now you can use styles on metadata that contain URLs! Typify will seamlessly replace the long link with your "pill" text while keeping the click functionality intact. (Fully supported even in the *Bases* Cards view).
- **рҹҳҖ Native Emoji Support:** Want to use emojis instead of SVG icons? Now you can! We've added a new tab in the icon picker with _Fuzzy Search_ support (smart search by synonyms) for you to find the perfect emoji.
- **рҹ“Ұ Massive Lucide Icons Update:** Typify's internal dictionary was drastically expanded (from 1727 to ~1957 icons). Discover around 230 new icons, such as utility symbols, new vehicles, and even zodiac signs in your _autocomplete_. 

*(Note: the corresponding SVGs will become visible as Obsidian natively incorporates them in future app updates)*.

## рҹҺЁ UI / UX Improvements

- **Color Indicator (Style Manager):** The old tiny circular color dot (`вҖў`) was replaced by an elegant and clean **continuous vertical bar** on the side of the item, freeing up more reading space and making the interface look much more sophisticated.
- **Smart Dropdown Menu:** The style management tab now features a clean _Dropdown_ filter (scope selector), making it easy to filter between global styles or specific properties, reducing visual clutter.
- **Modern Color Picker:** We updated the old circular picker to a more elegant rounded square, consistent with Obsidian's modern native standard.
- **Real-Time Preview:** Your Style Manager now perfectly displays real-time _previews_ for both native emojis and custom images, instantly reacting as you save configurations.
- **Mobile Compatibility:** We fixed the visuals of the Import/Export buttons and textboxes (*textarea*) on mobile, which previously had distorted borders due to native CSS injections from iOS/Android systems.

## рҹӣ  Architecture and Security (Bug Fixes)

- **Bases View Visual Fix:** Custom images now correctly respect grid margins (`4px`) in card views, eliminating bizarre spacing issues.
- **Security Shield (CSS Injection):** We refactored the Emoji and String processing engine to ensure 100% security and sanitization against the malicious or accidental execution of CSS (`fix(security)`).
- **Synchronized Translations:** Full expansion of dictionary keys (i18n) to accommodate the new Emoji interface in Spanish, French, Chinese, and Portuguese.
- **Deep Clean:** The source folder (`src/`) was completely restructured for modularity and all Linting warnings were resolved, ensuring clean, fast, and maintainable code.

---
*Thank you for using Typify!*

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
Typify 1.3.3 рҹҺЁ

A compatibility patch bumping the plugin version and aligning specifications with Obsidian's modern rendering standards.

## Why the sudden version bump?

- **рҹҡҖ Elevated Minimum Obsidian Version (`minAppVersion`):** The minimum application version has been elevated to **Obsidian 1.5.3** (which runs on Chromium 120).
- **рҹӣЎпёҸ Pristine CSS Mask Compatibility:** Modern standard CSS properties like `css-masks` (which Typify uses to dynamically colorize custom status icons) are fully supported natively and without vendor-prefixed requirements starting from Chromium 120 (Obsidian 1.5.3+). 
- Raising the minimum version ensures **100% clean compatibility** and clears all legacy validation warnings flagged by the Obsidian Community Plugin automated linter, guaranteeing a smooth and standard review process.
- **вңЁ Legacy Compatibility:** There is no functional drawback to this shift, as nearly 100% of active Obsidian users are already running versions 1.6+ or 1.7+. All the latest visual, performance, and UI refinements introduced in `1.3.2` are fully carried over and active in this release!

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
Typify 1.3.2 рҹҺЁ

A comprehensive update bringing UI refinements, performance optimizations, DOM resilience, and more compatibility for the Minimal theme.

## What's Changed

### рҹӣ пёҸ UI & Visual Polish
* **рҹ”— Elegant Link Styling in Pills:** External links (such as URLs used as tag values) now inherit the custom pill's text color and remove the default blue/underlined style. They keep a clean `pointer` cursor and display a subtle underline only on hover to maintain visual coherence.
* **вңӮпёҸ Text Truncation in Previews:** Text in the Style Editor Modal preview pills now uses ellipsis (`...`) when long values are entered, preventing the card container background from clipping or breaking the layout.

### рҹҺЁ Minimal Theme Compatibility
* **рҹҺҜ Scoped Property Sizing:** Fixed a quirk where the reduced tag font-size for the Minimal theme was applying globally. Sizing is now scoped strictly to the tight vertical spaces of the Bases Cards View (`.bases-view`). Pills in the standard Properties/Metadata editor now remain at their full, beautifully proportioned size.
* **рҹ“җ Border-Radius Decoupling:** Decoupled Typify's border-radius styling from Minimal's native variables, guaranteeing that customized tag formats (pill, rectangle, flat) render as intended regardless of local Minimal Style Settings.
* **рҹҡ« Prevented Tag Clipping:** Fixed clipping at the bottom of tags inside the Bases Cards view specifically when using the Minimal theme.
* **вҡ–пёҸ Normalized Font Sizes:** Corrected font size scaling inside card lists on the Minimal theme to keep proportional rendering.

### вҡЎ Performance & DOM Resilience
* **рҹӣЎпёҸ Smart Stylesheet Re-attaching:** Fixed a bug where workspace layout reloads could silently strip Typify's custom `<style>` element from the DOM. The plugin now automatically detects and re-attaches its stylesheet safely.
* **рҹҡҖ Optimized Style Processing:** Refactored the style manager cache to eliminate redundant styling rebuilds, significantly reducing DOM mutation overhead.

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.3.1 рҹҺЁ

## Bug Fixes

рҹ“җ **Mobile Bases Cards Layout**
Fixed an issue where styled pills in the Bases Cards view were getting clipped at the bottom on mobile devices.

## Compliance

вң… **Obsidian Review Bot вҖ” All `!important` Removed**
Removed all 9 `!important` declarations from image tag styles in `styles.css`, as flagged by the ObsidianReviewBot. Selector specificity was already high enough to guarantee correct rendering without them.

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.3.0 рҹҺЁ

## New Features

рҹ–јпёҸ **Local Custom Images (Avatars)**
You can now use custom local images (PNG, JPG, etc.) as your tag icons! This lets you create Notion-like вҖңContactвҖқ tags with user avatars or any custom graphic, but the Obsidian way.
- Images are managed securely via a dedicated folder (`.obsidian/plugins/typify/img/`).
- Fully supported across standard properties, lists, and Bases Cards.
- *(Note: Images must be added manually outside of Obsidian into the folder).*

рҹ‘ҒпёҸ **Hide the "Remove" Button (X)**
Added a highly requested setting to hide the native Obsidian "remove property" button (the 'x' icon) from pills. This gives your properties a clean, aesthetic "read-only badge" look.
You can selectively hide it:
- Everywhere
- Only in Obsidian's native Properties view
- Only in Bases plugin views

рҹҺЁ **Redesigned Icon Picker Modal**
The Icon Picker modal has been completely revamped into a seamless tabbed interface. You can now easily switch between the native **Lucide Icons**, your **Custom SVG Icons**, and your new **Custom Images**, all with built-in search functionality.

## Bug Fixes & Improvements

вҷҫпёҸ **Obsidian Canvas Dynamic Rendering**
Fixed a stubborn issue where Typify styles wouldn't appear initially inside Obsidian Canvas nodes. 
- Replaced fragile DOM observers with a hyper-optimized, lightweight polling system.
- Typify now flawlessly detects and styles lazy-loaded cards or newly dragged files in your Canvas dynamically, with absolutely zero impact on scrolling/dragging performance.

## Internal & Compliance

рҹӣЎпёҸ **Strict Code Quality Audit**
Ran a comprehensive `strictTypeChecked` ESLint audit across the entire codebase to improve plugin stability:
- Resolved 26 high-priority violations.
- Converted dangerous non-null assertions (`!`) to safe optional chaining (`?.`) across the DOM Managers.
- Ensured safe template literal coercion and properly scoped void callbacks to prevent memory leaks during plugin unload.

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.2.0 рҹҺЁ

## New Features

### рҹҺЁ Solid Color Mode
Added a new **Color mode** option per style with two choices: **Subtle** (default) and **Solid**.

- **Subtle** вҖ” The original translucent look using semi-transparent backgrounds (alpha 0.2вҖ“0.35) that blend seamlessly with any theme.
- **Solid** вҖ” Vibrant, fully opaque colors with boosted saturation and calibrated contrast. Backgrounds use ~72% lightness in light mode and ~35% in dark mode for a bold, eye-catching look вҖ” similar to tags in Notion or Linear.

Each style can be independently configured. Existing styles are unaffected вҖ” they default to Subtle.

### рҹ“Ӣ Clipboard-Based Export & Import
Replaced the previous file-based export/import system with a cleaner, clipboard-powered approach.

**Export** вҖ” Opens a modal displaying your configuration as formatted JSON in a read-only textarea. One click copies everything to your clipboard вҖ” no files created in your vault.

**Import** вҖ” Opens a modal where you paste JSON directly. The plugin validates every single style before applying:
- Required fields (`name`, `baseColor` as valid `#RRGGBB` hex)
- Optional fields (`shape`, `colorMode`, `icon`, `appliesTo`) are type-checked against allowed values
- Invalid styles are silently filtered вҖ” you'll see exactly how many were imported vs. skipped

This change makes sharing configurations via Discord, Reddit, or any messaging platform as simple as copy-paste. It also improves mobile compatibility by removing the file picker dependency.

---

## Compliance

### вң… Obsidian Review Bot вҖ” All Issues Resolved
Addressed all 34 issues flagged by the ObsidianReviewBot automated scan:

**Risks (6/6 fixed)**
- Removed the `eslint-disable` directive and forbidden `<style>` element bypass вҖ” replaced with Obsidian's `createEl('style')`
- Bumped `minAppVersion` to `1.1.0` to match usage of `ColorComponent.setValue/onChange` (v1.0.0+) and `ButtonComponent.setIcon/setTooltip` (v1.1.0+)

**Warnings (25/26 fixed, 1 upstream false positive)**
- Replaced all 10 `document` references with `activeDocument` for popout window compatibility
- Replaced all 7 `instanceof HTMLElement` checks with `.instanceOf(HTMLElement)` for cross-window safety
- Replaced all 4 `instanceof SVGElement` checks with `.instanceOf(SVGElement)`
- Replaced `document.createElement('span')` with `createSpan()`
- Replaced `document.createElement('input')` with `createEl('input')` *(now fully removed with the new clipboard import)*
- Replaced `localStorage.getItem('language')` with Obsidian's native `getLanguage()` API

**Other (2/2 fixed)**
- Added GitHub Actions release workflow with `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2` for artifact attestation on `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json`

---

## CI/CD

### рҹ”§ Automated Release Workflow
New `.github/workflows/release.yml` that triggers on GitHub release creation:
- Validates that `manifest.json` version matches the release tag
- Builds the production bundle via `npm ci` + `npm run build`
- Generates build provenance attestation for all release assets
- Automatically uploads `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` to the release

---

## Internal
- Removed 1 unnecessary type assertion (`as HTMLStyleElement`) flagged by ESLint
- All source files pass `eslint`, `tsc --strict`, and `esbuild` production build with zero errors/warnings
- Localization updated across all 5 languages (EN, PT-BR, ES, FR, ZH-CN) with 9 new translation keys

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.1.3 вҡЎ

## Performance

### рҹҡҖ Global CSS Stylesheet вҖ” Elimination of Inline Styles
Replaced all per-element inline style injections (`el.style.setProperty(...)`) with a single dynamic `<style>` tag injected into the document `<head>`.

**Before:** Every pill on screen received individual `style="background-color:...; color:..."` attributes, forcing the browser to recalculate layout (reflow) for each element independently.

**After:** The `StyleManager` now generates one centralized stylesheet (`<style id="typify-dynamic-styles">`) containing all user-configured rules as proper CSS classes (e.g., `.typify-style-0`). Pills simply receive a `classList.add()` call, which the browser processes almost instantly.

**Impact:** ~73% reduction in Typify function call time (27.4 ms вҶ’ 7.2 ms), with up to ~3.8Г— faster rendering in real-world profiling.

### рҹҡҖ O(1) Style Lookup Dictionary
Eliminated sequential `.find()` array scans when matching pill values to configured styles.

**Before:** For every pill rendered, the plugin iterated through the entire `statusStyles` array to find a match вҖ” O(n) per pill.

**After:** A pre-computed `Map` (`fastLookupMap`) is built once on settings load, mapping `value|property` keys directly to their CSS class names вҖ” O(1) per pill.

### рҹҡҖ Delta-Only MutationObserver
Removed the global `debouncedRefresh()` full-document scan that triggered on every DOM change.

**Before:** Any mutation scheduled a debounced full scan of `document.body`, causing visible jank when scrolling large tables.

**After:** The `MutationObserver` now processes only the specific nodes added or changed (delta processing), leaving the rest of the DOM untouched.

## Refactoring

### рҹҸ—пёҸ Architectural Decoupling вҖ” `main.ts` Reduced from 641 to ~85 Lines
The monolithic `main.ts` was split into dedicated modules following Obsidian community best practices:

- **`StyleManager`** (`src/style-manager.ts`) вҖ” Owns all CSS generation, the global stylesheet lifecycle, and the O(1) lookup cache.
- **`DOMManager`** (`src/dom-manager.ts`) вҖ” Owns the `MutationObserver`, workspace event listeners, and all pill/element processing logic.
- **`main.ts`** вҖ” Now focuses exclusively on plugin lifecycle (`onload`, `onunload`, settings).

### рҹ§№ Settings UI Cleanup
Removed the redundant plugin title heading (with version badge) from the settings tab for a cleaner, flatter interface. Section dividers (Configuration, Styles, Data Management) are preserved.

## Bug Fixes

### рҹ©№ Folio Plugin Compatibility вҖ” `processPill` API Restored
Fixed a regression where the [Obsidian-Folio](https://github.com/Leike-Dev/Obsidian-Folio) plugin could no longer style pills in its metadata header.

**Root cause:** The `processPill` method was moved from `main.ts` into `DOMManager` during refactoring, breaking the public API that Folio calls via `typify.processPill(pill, propertyKey)`.

**Fix:** Added a bridge method on the plugin class that delegates to `DOMManager.processPill()`, restoring full cross-plugin compatibility without requiring any changes to Folio.

## Internal
- Fixed 2 unnecessary type assertions (`as HTMLElement`) flagged during final ESLint pass
- All source files pass `tsc --strict` and `esbuild` production build with zero errors

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.1.1 рҹӣ пёҸ

## Bug Fixes

### рҹ©№ Bases Cards View вҖ” Single-Value Properties

Fixed an issue where styled pills were **not rendered** in the Bases Cards view when a list property contained only a single value.

**Root cause:** The Bases plugin uses different DOM structures depending on the number of values:
- **Multiple values** вҶ’ wraps each in a `.value-list-element` вң… (already handled)
- **Single value** вҶ’ renders as plain text inside `.bases-rendered-value` вқҢ (was not handled)

**Fix:** Added `processSingleCardValue()`, which wraps single-value text in a `<span class="typify-single-value">` before applying styles. This preserves the card layout container without breaking alignment.

### рҹ©№ Bases Cards View вҖ” Mobile Clipping (Resolved)

As a side effect of the fix above, **the known issue of pills being clipped at the bottom on Obsidian Mobile is now resolved**. The previous approach styled the card line container directly, which inflated its dimensions and caused overflow. The new wrapper span approach keeps the container intact.

The "Known Issues" section has been **removed from all 5 READMEs** since the workaround is no longer needed.

## Internal

- Fixed 2 ESLint errors (`@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion`)
- Added `.npmrc` with `tag-version-prefix=""` to prevent `v` prefix on future version tags

---

**Full Changelog:** [1.1.0...1.1.1](https://github.com/Leike-Dev/Obsidian-Typify/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1)

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
## рҹҺЁ Typify 1.1.0

### вңЁ New Features

- **Flat Shape**: New shape option with `border-radius: 0px` for a sharp, clean look вҖ” alongside the existing Pill and Rectangle shapes.

- **рҹҢҚ 3 New Languages**: Full localization support added for:
  - рҹҮӘрҹҮё Spanish (EspaГұol)
  - рҹҮ«рҹҮ· French (FranГ§ais)
  - рҹҮЁрҹҮі Simplified Chinese (з®ҖдҪ“дёӯж–Ү)

### рҹӣ пёҸ Improvements

- **Style Manager**: Styles are now grouped by scope for better organization and easier management.
- **TypeScript**: Updated from 4.7 вҶ’ 5.7
- **ESLint**: Migrated from ESLint v8 (`.eslintrc.js`) to ESLint v9.39 (flat config `eslint.config.mjs`)

### рҹ“„ Documentation

- Translated READMEs for all new languages
- Updated language switcher across all README files

### рҹ”§ Internal

- Updated `version-bump.mjs` to include new README files in badge sync
- Removed legacy `.eslintrc.js`

---

**Full Changelog**: `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`

releases.author: Leike-Dev
releases.description:
# Typify 1.0.0 рҹҺү

First public release of Typify вҖ” a plugin that creates attractive and customizable status indicators with Lucide icons for your metadata properties.

## вңЁ Features
- **Customizable status pills** with color palette generation (light & dark mode)
- **Lucide icon integration** with searchable icon picker
- **Shape options**: Pill or Rectangle
- **Custom SVG icons** support
- **Per-property scoping** вҖ” styles can target specific properties or all
- **Style Manager** вҖ” create, edit, reorder, and delete styles
- **Import/Export** вҖ” backup and share your configurations
- **Mobile compatible** вҖ” works on Obsidian mobile
- **Multi-language ready** вҖ” i18n architecture with English included

## рҹ§№ Code Quality
- Zero ESLint errors (with `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`)
- Fully compliant with Obsidian community plugin review standards
- No `innerHTML`, no forbidden elements, no redundant assertions
- CSS-only styling via `styles.css` (no dynamic `<style>` injection)
